content: Treatment based on ASCVD risk stratification. Statins first-line for LDL lowering: high-intensity (atorvastatin 40-80mg, rosuvastatin 20-40mg) for clinical ASCVD or high-risk primary prevention; moderate-intensity for intermediate risk. Add ezetimibe if LDL remains above threshold on maximally tolerated statin. PCSK9 inhibitors (alirocumab, evolocumab) or inclisiran (siRNA) for FH or statin-intolerant patients not at goal. Bempedoic acid alternative for statin-intolerant. For severe hypertriglyceridemia (≥500 mg/dL): fibrates (fenofibrate preferred over gemfibrozil with statins), prescription omega-3 fatty acids (icosapent ethyl - REDUCE-IT trial showed CVD benefit; omega-3 acid ethyl esters), niacin rarely used now. Lifestyle foundation: Mediterranean diet, reduce saturated/trans fats, increase soluble fiber, weight loss, regular exercise, limit alcohol (raises triglycerides). FH requires aggressive early treatment, often combination therapy, cascade screening of relatives.
